text.title: Django Services Layer Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: View body contains 60+ lines of business logic across 3 models; nothing in services.py.
antipattern.root-cause: Logic written inline because 'services felt like overengineering'.
antipattern.fix: Extract into entity_action service; view becomes 5-line dispatch.

antipattern.symptom: Email task fires for an order that doesn't yet exist; rollback leaves an orphan email sent.
Services named create_order, update_user, etc. Project mixes both conventions.
antipattern.fix: Rename to order_create, user_update; document the convention in project AGENTS.md.

antipattern.symptom: Every service is wrapped in @transaction.atomic, including read-only.
antipattern.fix: Remove @atomic from single-write and read-only services.
